title: elymus dahuricus subsp. excelsus
intro: Elymus dahuricus subsp. excelsus, also known as Dahurian wildrye, is a type of flowering plant in the grass family, characterized by its tall stems and feathery plumes. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Elymus dahuricus subsp. excelsus include reducing fever, relieving pain, and improving respiratory conditions such as bronchitis and asthma. Elymus dahuricus subsp. excelsus can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are typically consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
